Shauna Schramke.short: Shauna Schramke paints old, quirky homes as portraits—introverted, slightly off, with strong opinions and tender hearts.
Shauna Schramke.medium: Shauna Schramke transforms old, quirky homes into timescapes and portals. Her houses are portraits of the introverted and slightly off—dwellings with strong opinions and tender hearts that refuse to let go.
Shauna Schramke.long: Shauna Schramke paints old, quirky homes as portraits of personality. Her houses are timescapes and portals—introverted, slightly off, with strong opinions and tender hearts. She captures dwellings that refuse to let go or move out of the way, celebrating painted wood and out-of-plumb foundations with soul.

Emily von Stuckrad-Smolinski.short: Emily von Stuckrad-Smolinski sculpts oil paint with unconventional tools, creating textured works full of energy.
Emily von Stuckrad-Smolinski.medium: Emily von Stuckrad-Smolinski transforms oil painting through texture and unconventional tools. Her works breathe with energy as palette knives, fingers, and even air compressors replace traditional brushes.
Emily von Stuckrad-Smolinski.long: Emily von Stuckrad-Smolinski pushes beyond traditional brushwork, using palette knives, fingers, and air compressors to sculpt richly textured oil paintings. A theatre professional by day, she compares her creative process to running a race, building momentum until each piece comes alive with its own energy.

Kurt Smolinski.short: Kurt channels color through fluid pouring techniques refined by persistent experimentation and an eye for excellence.
Kurt Smolinski.medium: Kurt channels color through fluid pouring techniques refined by persistent experimentation. This middle schooler balances his artistic practice with tennis and pickleball, letting only his finest work shine.
Kurt Smolinski.long: Kurt channels color through fluid pouring techniques refined through persistent experimentation and an unwavering eye for excellence. This middle school artist lets the colors guide his creative process, balancing his studio practice with tennis and pickleball. Only his finest work makes the cut.

Linda Hart.short: Linda Hart transforms decaying industrial buildings and garden blooms into luminous watercolors.
Linda Hart.medium: Linda Hart captures hidden beauty in crumbling warehouses and vibrant florals through watercolor. Her work transforms decay and nature into ethereal abstract compositions that reveal overlooked splendor.
Linda Hart.long: Linda Hart finds beauty in unlikely places—decaying Dayton warehouses and garden blooms become luminous watercolors. Her Chasing Urban Shadows series celebrates industrial decay, while Floral Auras honors her nursery roots. Both capture time's transformative power through masterful watermedia technique.

Ching Chung.short: Ching creates bright, whimsical digital art inspired by her Asian heritage and design background.
Ching Chung.medium: Ching translates her visual world into joyful digital art. Drawing from her Bruneian roots and Industrial Design training, she captures inspired moments in bright, playful colors.
Ching Chung.long: Ching transforms curiosity and inspired moments into bright, whimsical digital art. Her Bruneian heritage and seamstress mother instilled a love of color and design, while her Industrial Design degree shaped her visual thinking. She captures joy and meaning in every colorful piece.

Dave Crowell.short: A lifelong artist who transforms concepts into reality through any medium, driven by passion for human expression.
Dave Crowell.medium: A lifelong artist and designer who founded Custom Frame Services, Inc in 1993. Transforms concepts into authentic creative expressions while helping fellow artists realize their visions.
Dave Crowell.long: A lifelong artist and designer who transforms concepts through any available medium. Founded Custom Frame Services, Inc in 1993 to help artists realize their visions. Views art as a social engine, drawing on history and philosophy to create authentic expressions of human experience.

Bonnie Kuntz.short: Bonnie embraces art's uncertainty, painting animals, florals & landscapes in watercolor, acrylics & mixed media.
Bonnie Kuntz.medium: Bonnie teaches watercolor while exploring unpredictable techniques across multiple mediums. Her subjects range from horses to florals, celebrating art's adventurous uncertainty much like life itself.
Bonnie Kuntz.long: Retired RN and active art educator Bonnie paints horses, animals, florals and landscapes in watercolor, acrylics and mixed media. She thrives on art's unpredictability, exploring new subjects and techniques. Her work appears at The Artery, Gem City Catfe, and Springfield Museum of Art.

Marlene Steele.short: Bold, vibrant painter who transforms salvaged materials into art, embracing pure spontaneity and discovery.
Marlene Steele.medium: This artist upcycles building salvage into vibrant paintings, working without plans and letting each piece evolve spontaneously. Every brushstroke is guided by intuition and the thrill of creative discovery.
Marlene Steele.long: This visionary painter transforms salvaged building materials into bold, vibrant art pieces through pure intuition. Working without predetermined plans, each painting evolves organically, shaped by mood and moment. The result is unpredictable, exciting work that celebrates spontaneity and the beauty of creative discovery.

Jo Anne Vincent.short: Jo Anne Vincent creates sterling silver and copper jewelry combining vibrant beads, pearls, and gemstones.
Jo Anne Vincent.medium: Jo Anne Vincent crafts distinctive sterling silver and copper jewelry, combining pearls, hand-blown glass, agate, turquoise, and Balinese beads. Each piece expresses her artistic vision through color and texture.
Jo Anne Vincent.long: Jo Anne Vincent transforms sterling silver and copper into distinctive jewelry that celebrates color, pattern, and texture. She selects pearls, hand-blown California glass, Balinese beaded beads, agate, turquoise, and lapis to create pieces that stand alone. Her 13 years of jewelry-making reflect an artistic vision.

Alison Bour.short: Alison Bour transforms her photography into stunning digital art, blending traditional and abstract styles.
Alison Bour.medium: Alison Bour combines photography with digital artistry to create both traditional nature scenes and bold abstracts. She uses only her own images—never AI—as the foundation for her work.
Alison Bour.long: Alison Bour transforms her original photographs into captivating digital art, creating everything from nature scenes to abstract pieces. She never uses AI, relying solely on her own images. Her work has appeared in juried shows and galleries across Ohio, including Dayton's Art in the City.

Ariel White.short: Ariel White transforms animals into whimsical art with bright colors and cartoon style, sparking joy and playfulness.
Ariel White.medium: Ariel White brings animals to life through vibrant, cartoon-style paintings. Her whimsical work celebrates the cute, silly, and odd, inviting viewers to laugh and play along with nature's charm.
Ariel White.long: Ariel White combines her love of nature and genetics background to create vibrant, cartoon-style animal paintings. Her whimsical art celebrates the cute, silly, and odd aspects of wildlife, aiming to spark joy and playfulness in viewers. She maintains a studio at Dayton Front Street.

Michelle Brandt.short: Self-taught artist creating vibrant acrylics & watercolors inspired by nature, bringing joy through bold color.
Michelle Brandt.medium: Self-taught artist working in acrylics and watercolors, transforming garden flowers and birds into impressionistic works. Every painting radiates joy through vibrant, happy colors and abstracted realism.
Michelle Brandt.long: Self-taught artist creating impressionistic acrylics and watercolors inspired by nature's vibrant beauty. From garden flowers to backyard birds, each painting celebrates bold, joyful color. A Dayton Society of Artists member bringing happiness to canvas, paper, and even rain barrels with award-winning work.

Michael Surber.short: Michael Surber creates poetic, primal paintings with vibrant color that honor tradition while pushing boundaries.
Michael Surber.medium: Michael Surber is an award-winning Ohio artist whose experimental paintings blend vibrant color with bold textures. His poetic work invites viewers to find their own stories within each piece.
Michael Surber.long: Michael Surber is an award-winning Ohio artist carving a unique niche in contemporary painting. His work blends vibrant color with bold textures in poetic, primal compositions. Collectors describe his experimental pieces as essential storytelling that honors tradition while pushing boundaries.

Nancy Shuler.short: Nancy Shuler creates stunning art through bold color, texture, and design that invites viewers to pause and appreciate.
Nancy Shuler.medium: Nancy Shuler combines color, texture, and design to create stunning pieces where dreams intertwine with reality. Her work transforms the ordinary into moments of profound beauty and reflection.
Nancy Shuler.long: Nancy Shuler is a full-time artist in Troy, Ohio, who combines color, texture, and design to create stunning works. Her art invites viewers to pause and appreciate where dreams intertwine with reality. Through paint and shared experiences, she reveals life's splendor and art's transformative power.

Kim Ceccarelli.short: Kim Ceccarelli captures light and shadow in oils and pastels, painting en plein air across diverse landscapes.
Kim Ceccarelli.medium: Kim Ceccarelli is an award-winning pastel and oil painter who works en plein air. Her vibrant work celebrates light, shadow, and the profound beauty found in everyday scenes across Ohio and beyond.
Kim Ceccarelli.long: Kim Ceccarelli paints in oils and pastels, capturing the interplay of light and shadow en plein air. An award-winning artist with work at the Smithsonian and galleries nationwide, she transformed from critical care nurse to full-time painter, fostering connections between viewers and beauty.

Cindy Callahan.short: Cindy is a self-taught painter creating textured acrylic and watercolor works that celebrate color, form, and depth.
Cindy Callahan.medium: Cindy is a prolific self-taught painter working in acrylics and watercolor. Her textured works celebrate the dynamic relationship between color, form, and depth using recycled objects and mixed mediums.
Cindy Callahan.long: Cindy is a Dayton-based self-taught artist and prolific painter in acrylics and watercolor. Her textured acrylic works celebrate color, form, and depth through innovative use of recycled objects and mixed mediums. Active in Dayton Society of Artists and Women's TriArt Society, her work has been featured in local galleries.

Lori Daugherty.short: Lori Daugherty creates bold abstract art with encaustic wax, building intricate colored layers that invite exploration.
Lori Daugherty.medium: Lori Daugherty builds intricate layers of color using encaustic wax, pigment, and shellac. Her bold abstracts invite viewers on an enchanted journey through texture and depth at her Dayton studio, Darty Art.
Lori Daugherty.long: Lori Daugherty transforms molten wax into bold, colorful abstracts using torches, hot plates, and encaustic techniques. Each piece features intricate layers that invite viewers to explore texture, color, and depth. Her work appears in collections across the US and China, and she welcomes visitors to Darty Art in Dayton, Ohio.

Pat McClelland.short: C. Pat McClelland explores binary relationships in art, transforming simple dualities into complex visual dialogues.
Pat McClelland.medium: C. Pat McClelland builds complex paintings from binary relationships—warm/cool, order/chaos, shape/line. Each work emerges through layered conversations between opposing elements, where loss and discovery yield completion.
Pat McClelland.long: C. Pat McClelland transforms binary relationships into dynamic visual complexity. Working with dualities like warm/cool, order/chaos, and shape/line, this Ohio artist builds layered compositions through an evolving dialogue. The challenge lies in sacrificing early developments for new strata, where loss and discovery ultimately yield completion.

Barbara Mandell.short: Barbara Mandell creates colorful impressionist and abstract art, donating proceeds to MS and breast cancer research.
Barbara Mandell.medium: Barbara Mandell blends impressionism, geometric shapes, and bold color in her original designs. A portion of all sales supports Multiple Sclerosis and Breast Cancer Research.
Barbara Mandell.long: Barbara Mandell brings 47 years of artistic vision to colorful, imaginative works that blend impressionism with architectural lines and geometric forms. Her original hand-created designs add vibrant energy to any space while supporting Multiple Sclerosis and Breast Cancer Research through partial proceeds.

Lindsey Tribelhorn.short: Acrylic painter creating portals to alternate realities that challenge perception and ignite visual transcendence.
Lindsey Tribelhorn.medium: Dayton-based acrylic painter whose work evokes hallucination without drugs, challenging perception and awakening heightened awareness. Each piece invites visual transcendence into alternate realities.
Lindsey Tribelhorn.long: Dayton-based artist specializing in acrylic painting, murals, and mixed media. Work explores sensory perception boundaries, creating portals to alternate realities that evoke hallucination without drugs. Each piece challenges conventional perception and invites viewers on journeys of visual transcendence.

Roxanne Grooms.short: Rich Novak layers circles with earthy hues and tactile stitches, inviting you to explore his imaginative compositions.
Roxanne Grooms.medium: Rich Novak transforms the circle into a dynamic character within his work, layering earthly hues with nurturing stitches and twisted threads that anchor each story and invite imaginative exploration.
Roxanne Grooms.long: Rich Novak brings decades of artistic training to his compelling mixed-media work, where circles become powerful characters among rich earthly hues and tactile stitches. His compositions blend soft gentleness with dynamic strength, inviting viewers to engage their imagination and discover personal stories within each piece.

Larry Hensel.short: Larry Hensel transforms architectural and historical photography into striking graphic design art.
Larry Hensel.medium: Larry Hensel blends 30+ years of graphic design expertise with photography, transforming historical architecture and vintage subjects into bold, eye-catching poster art.
Larry Hensel.long: Larry Hensel merges graphic design mastery with contemporary photography, capturing historical architecture, vintage aircraft, and local landscapes. His work transforms these images into striking architectural panels and bold poster designs that celebrate heritage with a modern eye.

Brian Mathus.short: Brian paints with emotional clarity, removing distortion to reveal the sublime in figures, landscapes, and life.
Brian Mathus.medium: Dayton artist Brian strips away visual distortion to paint with emotional clarity. His vibrant figurative works layer acrylic and oil, blending Fauvism and African art into ecstatic compositions.
Brian Mathus.long: Brian creates figurative paintings that pursue emotional clarity by removing imposed distortions in how we see. The Dayton artist layers acrylic and oil in vibrant Fauvist compositions, integrating cubism and African art. His work builds toward the ecstatic and sublime, one luminous layer at a time.

Mikee Huber.short: Mikee Huber creates abstract art using pipettes and scrapers, transforming data into vibrant color fields.
Mikee Huber.medium: Mikee Huber transforms scientific imagery and topographic views into vibrant abstractions. Using pipettes and scrapers, she creates dynamic color fields exploring human connection and community.
Mikee Huber.long: Mikee Huber blends intuition with visual data in her abstract paintings. Inspired by circuit boards, music, and urban diversity, she uses unconventional tools to create vibrant color fields. Her Connections series explores human bonds through intersecting lines, celebrating unity and community.

Pam Geisel.short: Pam Geisel creates bold graphic art quilts, from abstract pieced designs to socio-political works with typography.
Pam Geisel.medium: Pam Geisel brings her graphic design expertise to art quilts, creating both abstract geometric pieces and landscape works. She explores socio-political themes through typography and bold visual statements.
Pam Geisel.long: Pam Geisel combines her graphic design background with quilting to create striking abstract and representational works. Based in Yellow Springs, Ohio, she explores secondary patterns in traditional pieced quilts and increasingly incorporates typography to address socio-political issues through textile art.

Erin Smith Glenn.short: Erin Morgan Smith Glenn creates art driven by advocacy, HAIRitage, and mental health awareness through mixed media.
Erin Smith Glenn.medium: Erin Morgan Smith Glenn is an associate professor whose work explores advocacy, HAIRitage, and mental health. She exhibits nationally and internationally while leading study abroad programs.
Erin Smith Glenn.long: Erin Morgan Smith Glenn is an associate professor and former VP of the Dayton Society of Artists whose work centers on advocacy, activism, HAIRitage, and mental health awareness. She exhibits internationally, leads study abroad programs, and runs TheScarvinArtist LLC, creating commissions and mixed media designs.

Bruce Soifer.short: Award-winning photographer capturing fleeting moments of creation with the eye of a painter and heart of an elephant.
Bruce Soifer.medium: Award-winning photographer capturing creation's fleeting moments—from dancers leaping to sunrises blazing. With a painter's eye and an elephant's heart, this artist freezes passion, joy, and beauty.
Bruce Soifer.long: Award-winning photographer with a painter's eye and an elephant's heart, capturing creation's fleeting moments across all art forms. From dancers mid-leap to musicians lost in melody, from sculptors shaping clay to nature's blazing sunrises, this artist freezes the passion, beauty, and raw emotion of creative expression.

Alisa H. Workman.short: Alisa H. Workman transforms darkness into vibrant floral paintings that celebrate life through bold color and nature.
Alisa H. Workman.medium: Alisa H. Workman transforms personal loss into vibrant abstract florals. Her bold paintings burst with rich color, turning sadness into joy through nature-inspired works collected across the US and Europe.
Alisa H. Workman.long: Alisa H. Workman channels grief into celebration through vibrant abstract florals. A preschool art teacher and WSU geology graduate, she transforms darkness into bold paintings with rich splashes of color. Her nature-inspired works are collected throughout the US, Canada, and Europe.

Beth Borchers.short: Beth Borchers creates soul-stirring abstract art with intense colors and metallic accents that connect to your heart.
Beth Borchers.medium: Beth Borchers is a self-taught mixed media artist creating captivating abstract works with bold colors, playful swirls, and metallic accents. Each piece tells a unique story meant to stir emotion and joy.
Beth Borchers.long: Beth Borchers transforms nature's magic into soul-reaching abstract art with intense colors and bold, playful choices. This self-taught mixed media artist layers splashes, swirls, and metallic accents to create depth and emotion. Her captivating work reflects love, peace, and joy, with each piece telling its own story.

Amy Kollar Anderson.short: Amy Kollar Anderson layers glitter, mica, and foil into surreal paintings exploring nature, dreams, and transformation.
Amy Kollar Anderson.medium: Amy Kollar Anderson builds layered surfaces with glitter, mica, and metallic foils, creating surreal narrative paintings that explore transformation, anxiety, and environmental concerns through dreamlike imagery.
Amy Kollar Anderson.long: Amy Kollar Anderson creates surreal narrative paintings by layering pouring medium embedded with glitter, mica, washi, and holographic foils. Her work explores transformation, anxiety, and humor through nature-inspired imagery. Recently completing a 13-panel Zodiac series, she's now working on the Celtic Tree Zodiac.

Erica Keener.short: Erica captures clouds and storms in bold pastels, inviting viewers to find sanctuary in nature's fleeting beauty.
Erica Keener.medium: Erica transforms ordinary landscapes into sources of wonder through bold pastels. Her storm-filled skies and natural scenes offer viewers sanctuary, encouraging us all to look up more often.
Erica Keener.long: Erica uses bold pastels to capture storms moving across landscapes near her Preble County home. Her award-winning work transforms fleeting moments into sanctuary, offering viewers solace through nature's ever-changing skies. She invites everyone to look up and find wonder in the world around us.

Gretchen Durst Jacobs.short: Gretchen Durst Jacobs creates body-scaled abstract paintings exploring color, nature, and feminist perspectives.
Gretchen Durst Jacobs.medium: Gretchen Durst Jacobs creates body-scaled abstract paintings with geometric grids and expressive gestures. Her work explores the natural world, climate change, and reclaims abstract painting through a feminine lens.
Gretchen Durst Jacobs.long: Gretchen Durst Jacobs creates non-objective paintings scaled to her body, exploring color, movement, and space. Working within carefully designed grids, she layers intuitive gestures that embody landscape and examine our relationship with nature. Her practice reclaims abstract painting through a distinctly feminine perspective.

Lydia Williamson.short: Lydia Williamson creates whimsical 'NOODLE People'—long-limbed figures exploring human humor and awkwardness.
Lydia Williamson.medium: Lydia Williamson paints playful 'NOODLE People' with exaggerated limbs that explore human awkwardness. Her acrylic and mixed media work invites viewers to project their own meaning onto each spontaneous piece.
Lydia Williamson.long: Lydia Williamson transforms human experience into whimsical 'NOODLE People'—exaggerated, long-limbed figures that celebrate awkwardness and humor. Working in acrylics and mixed media from her Dayton gallery, she creates accessible, playful pieces that invite personal interpretation and connection through spontaneous, boundary-pushing expression.

Christy Veres.short: Christy Veres creates bohemian folk art using reclaimed materials, blending woodburning, acrylics, and mixed media.
Christy Veres.medium: Christy Veres transforms reclaimed wood, metals, and canvas into bohemian folk art. Working with acrylics, woodburning, and diverse media, she creates unique interpretations inspired by nature and fellow artists.
Christy Veres.long: Christy Veres crafts bohemian folk art by refurbishing reclaimed materials into one-of-a-kind pieces. Her work spans woodburning, carving, acrylics, and mixed media. Inspired by nature and artists like Georgia O'Keeffe and Keith Haring, she creates art for others' enjoyment at festivals and galleries throughout Ohio.

Cydnie King.short: Cydnie L. King merges human figures with botanicals using vivid inks and pastels to explore our bond with nature.
Cydnie King.medium: Cydnie L. King creates vivid works where human figures meld into botanicals, exploring humanity's inseparable connection to nature. Working with inks, pastels, and charcoals, she examines our bond with the natural world.
Cydnie King.long: Cydnie L. King depicts human figures melding into botanicals through vivid inks, pastels, and charcoals. Her work explores humanity's inseparable bond with nature and examines genetic modification's impact on plants. Inspired by her parents' love of flowers, she emphasizes that no one is immune to nature's forces.

Jeremy Nickoson.short: Jeremy captures nature's wonders through self-taught landscape and astrophotography, printed on vibrant metal.
Jeremy Nickoson.medium: Jeremy is a self-taught photographer specializing in landscapes and astrophotography from 56 National Parks. His metal prints capture nature's vibrancy, inspiring others to explore the outdoors.
Jeremy Nickoson.long: Jeremy is a self-taught nature photographer who has spent 15+ years capturing landscapes and night skies across 56 National Parks. His metal and metallic prints showcase incredible vibrancy, aiming to inspire others to explore nature's wonders. He also volunteers at John Glenn Astronomy Park sharing his passion for the night sky.

Tami Beale.short: Tami Beale creates bold abstract oil paintings with vibrant layers and geometric energy from her Ohio studio.
Tami Beale.medium: Tami Beale transforms canvas into layered abstract compositions using vibrant oils and mixed media. Her geometric works bring therapeutic beauty and contemporary boldness to every space.
Tami Beale.long: Tami Beale is a self-taught abstract artist with over two decades of experience creating vibrant oil and mixed media paintings. Her layered geometric compositions feature bold textures and contemporary energy. She believes in art's therapeutic power for both creator and viewer alike.

Glenna Jennings.short: Glenna Jennings photographs tables worldwide, transforming meals into cross-cultural family albums that explore connection.
Glenna Jennings.medium: Glenna Jennings captures global tablescapes, weaving strangers into chosen families. Her photography explores food, memory, and connection across borders while advancing food justice through art.
Glenna Jennings.long: Glenna Jennings transforms ordinary meals into extraordinary connections through her series At Table. Her photography documents gatherings across continents, creating a global family album. As educator and food justice activist, she bridges art and advocacy, earning NEA grants and international exhibition.

Libby Rudolf.short: This artist paints expressive watercolors capturing essence through color & form, working pleinair & from life.
Libby Rudolf.medium: This artist creates expressive watercolors that capture the essence of scenes and people through color and form. Their work is featured at Village Artisans Gallery in Yellow Springs, Ohio.
Libby Rudolf.long: This watercolor artist works expressively to capture the essence of scenes and people, weaving together color and form. They paint from life and pleinair, with work featured at Village Artisans co-op Gallery in Yellow Springs. They participate in group and solo shows and accept commissions.

Art of Frozen Time (Jeff Smith).short: Jeff Smith captures golden hour sunsets with panoramic precision, creating Art of Frozen Time for healing spaces.
Art of Frozen Time (Jeff Smith).medium: Jeff Smith chases fleeting sunsets, merging dozens of exposures into panoramic masterpieces. His Art of Frozen Time brings evidence-based healing through nature's most stunning light displays.
Art of Frozen Time (Jeff Smith).long: Jeff Smith transforms golden hour into Art of Frozen Time, capturing contre-jour sunsets that evoke awe and wonder. This former Air Force pilot merges up to 90 bracketed exposures into panoramic masterpieces designed for evidence-based healing spaces, sharing nature's brief spectacular moments of peace.

Matt Blair.short: Matt Blair captures Dayton's architecture and culture through plein air watercolors and urban sketches.
Matt Blair.medium: Matt Blair creates plein air watercolors and urban sketches celebrating Dayton's history, architecture, and culture. His playful yet realistic style brings the city's landmarks to life.
Matt Blair.long: Matt Blair brings Dayton to life through plein air watercolors and urban sketches that celebrate the city's rich history and architectural diversity. Working in both whimsical and realistic styles, he captures local landmarks, houses, and cultural scenes with warmth and authenticity.

Kathleen Caffrey.short: Anthrosercher explores history and culture through art, drawing inspiration from archaeology and forgotten techniques.
Kathleen Caffrey.medium: Anthrosercher weaves archaeological sites, historical artifacts, and cultural echoes into art that explores courage and transformation. Working across mediums from oil to watercolor, each piece reflects humanity's past.
Kathleen Caffrey.long: Anthrosercher creates art rooted in anthropology, archaeology, and cultural history. From Civil War sites to ancient artifacts, the work explores courage, transformation, and the complex reality of human experience. Working across traditional and experimental mediums, each piece connects past and present through emotional resonance.

Elisha Frontz.short: Elisha Frontz explores nature's patterns through sculpture and fiber art, revealing hidden mathematical beauty.
Elisha Frontz.medium: Elisha Frontz blends sculpture and fiber art to explore intricate patterns in nature and mathematics. Her detailed work reveals the hidden interconnectedness of all things through diverse techniques.
Elisha Frontz.long: Elisha Frontz is a Dayton-based artist who combines traditional sculpture with fiber art techniques to explore the complexity of natural and mathematical systems. Her intricate, detailed pieces reveal the hidden patterns and interconnectedness that unite the natural world with mathematical beauty.

Rebecca Rowley.short: Rebecca Rowley creates linocut prints inspired by nature and profound loss, blending bold ink with watercolor.
Rebecca Rowley.medium: Rebecca Rowley transforms grief into art through linocut prints of nature. Her work features bold ink over watercolor, capturing the creatures and landscapes of her Ohio woodlands home.
Rebecca Rowley.long: Rebecca Rowley channels her connection to Ohio's woodlands into linocut prints layered over watercolor. After her father's passing in 2021, surrounded by Monarch butterflies, she found her artistic voice. Working in collections of three, she creates pieces that forge deep connections with viewers.

Heather Caulfield.short: Heather Caulfield paints soulful figurative works, blending renaissance techniques with mystical, nature-inspired vision.
Heather Caulfield.medium: Heather Caulfield creates spiritually charged figurative paintings that capture the soul on canvas. Trained by master painters, she blends classical technique with mystical, nature-inspired expression.
Heather Caulfield.long: Heather Caulfield is a visionary figurative painter who captures the essence of the soul on canvas. Classically trained by master painters including Nelson Shanks, she creates spiritually charged works that blend renaissance techniques with mystical, nature-inspired imagery and whimsical expression.

Loura Lawrence.short: Loura captures vibrant details in nature and travel photography, crafting bold jewelry from gemstones and vintage finds.
Loura Lawrence.medium: Loura blends photojournalism training with artistic vision, capturing bold nature details and crafting distinctive gemstone jewelry. Her work spans travel photography to handmade pieces at CinnamonAndSilver.com.
Loura Lawrence.long: Loura transforms small subjects into vibrant art through her photojournalistic lens and jewelry craft. With 20+ years experience, she captures nature's details with bold colors while creating distinctive gemstone pieces. Her photography graces Creation Illustrated Magazine; her jewelry lives at CinnamonAndSilver.com.

Dayton Ohio Photos (Kevin Myers).short: John Smith captures Dayton Ohio through stunning night, architecture, and landscape photography since 2019.
Dayton Ohio Photos (Kevin Myers).medium: John Smith has photographed Dayton Ohio and beyond since 2019, specializing in night scenes, architecture, and landscapes. His mission: showcasing the region in its best light through striking imagery.
Dayton Ohio Photos (Kevin Myers).long: John Smith has been capturing Dayton Ohio since 2019, specializing in night photography, architecture, and landscapes. He shares his vision of the region in its best light through social media, revealing the beauty and character of Dayton and surrounding areas through his distinctive photographic perspective.

Christine Gaffney.short: Christine Gaffney sculpts with AI and her own body, challenging traditional gazes through anti-portraits and technology.
Christine Gaffney.medium: Christine Gaffney uses her body as sculptural material, collapsing maker and object. Her AI-generated anti-portraits and robotic sculptures challenge institutional gazes while exploring feminism and disability justice.
Christine Gaffney.long: Christine Gaffney creates interdisciplinary sculptures using AI, her own body, and robotic appliances. Her anti-portraits reveal the making process itself, challenging male institutional gazes. By generating datasets from her body, she controls her image while subverting gender expectations and exploring disability justice.

Abiona Venee.short: AV Glass blends hot shop and lampwork techniques in Dayton, creating unique glass art since 2007.
Abiona Venee.medium: AV Glass fuses hot shop glassblowing with lampwork expertise, crafting distinctive pieces from a Dayton studio. Dual apprenticeships inform every creation at Front Street Artist Studios.
Abiona Venee.long: AV Glass merges traditional hot shop glassblowing with intricate lampwork techniques, creating one-of-a-kind art since 2007. Two apprenticeships shaped this distinctive approach, now realized in a custom-built Dayton studio at Front Street Artist Studios, where technical mastery meets creative vision.

Kate Santucci.short: Kate Huser Santucci layers encaustic wax to explore human connection, nature, and communication through microbes and marks.
Kate Santucci.medium: Kate Huser Santucci uses encaustic painting to examine how humans connect with their environment. Her layered wax works feature microbes, plants, and asemic writing that invite viewers to look beneath the surface.
Kate Santucci.long: Kate Huser Santucci creates layered encaustic paintings exploring human interconnection with the natural world. Using images of microbes, plants, and asemic writing, she examines communication, time, and perception. Her textured wax surfaces invite viewers to glimpse imagery floating beneath, revealing stories of evolution.

Robert Farley.short: Award-winning photographer capturing landscapes, nature, and urban scenes from coast-to-coast travels across America.
Robert Farley.medium: This photographer transforms over a decade of cross-country travels into award-winning fine art images. Specializing in landscape, nature, and urban photography with recent honors at Ohio exhibitions.
Robert Farley.long: With over a decade capturing America's landscapes from coast to coast, this photographer turns wanderlust into award-winning fine art. Recent accolades include First Place for Amateur Photography at Tipp City and Second Place at Duveneck Art Show. Work displayed at The ARTery and exhibitions across Ohio.

Terry Welker.short: Terry Welker creates kinetic sculptures that dance with air and light, exploring motion through suspended forms.
Terry Welker.medium: Terry Welker, FAIA, transforms spaces with lightweight kinetic sculptures that respond to air currents. His suspended works animate architecture through motion, color, and delicate balance.
Terry Welker.long: Terry Welker, FAIA, bridges architecture and sculpture through kinetic installations that respond to natural forces. His suspended works in anodized aluminum and stainless steel explore motion, balance, and light. As architect and professor, he creates site-specific pieces that transform spaces into poetic experiences.
